methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pipedream operates in the Transportation sector at the acquired stage. The comparable universe consists of five private firms—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public market peers identified. The narrow peer set reflects the frontier nature of the sector and the rarity of same-stage, same-tier transactions.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable to this valuation. Despite identifying five Transportation sector peers, the dataset contains insufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables to generate a reliable median multiple. A minimum threshold of three datapoints within these narrow filters was not met, making broader all-sector medians inappropriate for this analysis.

Given the absence of quantifiable benchmarking methods, the valuation must rely on transaction context rather than multiple-based triangulation. Pipedream raised $22.4M in total capital and exited via acquisition. Without disclosed acquisition price, revenue multiples, or margin metrics from the peer set, the analysis cannot produce a defensible valuation range. The key uncertainties are (1) the acquisition consideration relative to total raised, (2) the revenue or growth trajectory at exit, and (3) whether any peer-set transaction data becomes available. A complete valuation narrative would require either the acquisition price or granular financial and exit data from one or more comparable companies.
